issues.title: Undefined utility class `nb-btn-press-md`
issues.description: The class `nb-btn-press-md` is used but never defined in utilities.css. Only `nb-btn-press-sm` and `nb-btn-press-lg` exist. This means the active press animation does not apply on the mobile bar CTA button.
issues.fix: Either add an `nb-btn-press-md` definition to `packages/ui/src/styles/utilities.css` (e.g., `translate: 2.5px 2.5px; box-shadow: none;`) or use one of the existing press variants (`nb-btn-press-sm` or `nb-btn-press-lg`).

issues.title: Dynamic Tailwind class via string interpolation in `hover:` prefix will not work
issues.description: The className uses `hover:${theme.bgLight}` which produces a string like `hover:bg-(--pink)/15`. Tailwind CSS cannot detect dynamic class names constructed via template literals at build time. This hover effect is silently broken and the FAQ button has no hover background.
issues.rule: tailwind.md - Tailwind JIT requires classes to appear as complete strings
issues.fix: Use inline style for the hover effect via a CSS custom property or use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` with state, or pre-generate the hover classes in the CATEGORY_THEMES constant (e.g., `hoverBg: 'hover:bg-(--pink)/15'`) so the full string is statically scannable.

issues.title: Colors use hex format instead of OKLCH
issues.description: The tailwind.md rule states colors should use OKLCH format (`oklch(...)`) but all CSS variables in colors.css use hex values (e.g., `--pink: #ff4890`). While this is functional, it does not follow the project's stated OKLCH preference. This is a shared package file affecting all apps.
issues.fix: Consider migrating color definitions to OKLCH format for wider gamut support and perceptual uniformity. This is a low-priority suggestion since hex values work correctly.

issues.title: Inline style for dynamic gradient background is justified
issues.description: ImageGallery and GalleryOverlays use inline `style={{ backgroundImage: ... }}` for dynamic CSS gradients and dot patterns. These are computed at runtime from theme variables and rotation angles, making inline styles the correct approach since Tailwind cannot handle fully dynamic gradient values.
issues.rule: tailwind.md - No inline styles (exception: dynamic computed values)
issues.fix: No action needed. Inline styles are appropriate here for dynamic computed values.

issues.title: Hardcoded candy color `bg-(--lilac)` for all author avatars
issues.description: The AuthorAvatar component always uses `bg-(--lilac)` regardless of the product category or review context. While not a bug, it could be more consistent if it used the section's category theme color like other components do.
issues.fix: Consider accepting a `theme` prop and using `theme.bg` for consistency with the rest of the product detail page. Low priority since the current approach is intentional design.

issues.title: Store app uses different body font than other apps
issues.description: The store uses `var(--font-dm-sans)` for body text while all other apps use `var(--font-syne)`. This is documented in the CSS comment ('DM Sans for body text (readable), Syne for display headings via .font-display') and appears intentional for the store's e-commerce readability needs.
issues.rule: css-consistency.md - CSS must be consistent across apps
issues.fix: No action needed if this is an intentional design decision. The font difference is documented and justified for the store's readability requirements.

issues.title: globals.css is properly synchronized across all apps
issues.description: The store's globals.css follows the same structure as all other apps: imports tailwindcss, tw-animate-css, ui/styles, includes @source directives for both ui and app-components packages, defines @custom-variant dark, and includes reduced-motion media query. CSS consistency is maintained.
issues.rule: css-consistency.md - All apps must have identical theme and styling

issues.title: Long className strings without cn() utility
issues.description: Multiple components (ProductCard, HeroSection, CartDrawer, etc.) use template literal string concatenation for conditional classes instead of the cn() utility. While functional, cn() provides cleaner conditional class handling and handles class conflicts via tailwind-merge.
issues.rule: tailwind.md - Use cn() for conditional classes
issues.fix: Consider using cn() from the shared package for conditional class composition. Example: `cn('flex border-[3px] border-foreground nb-shadow-md bg-background transition-transform hover:-translate-1', isFeatured ? 'flex-col sm:flex-row' : 'flex-col')`. Low priority since the current approach works correctly.

issues.title: Mobile-first responsive design correctly applied
issues.description: The ProductGrid and all product detail components consistently follow mobile-first responsive design. Base styles target mobile (e.g., `grid-cols-1`, `flex-col`), with breakpoint prefixes adding tablet/desktop layouts (`sm:grid-cols-2`, `lg:grid-cols-3`, `lg:flex-row`). This is correct usage.

issues.title: Category theme system uses custom CSS variable colors correctly
issues.description: The CATEGORY_THEMES constant maps product categories to Tailwind v4 CSS variable syntax (`bg-(--pink)`, `text-(--lilac)`, etc.). These variables are properly defined in the shared ui package's colors.css and mapped in theme.css. The approach is sound and theme-aware.
